reviews.review_text: nice and quiet. Clean facilities and hot shower. We spent two nights here and everything runs good. Close to the jetty where you can fish!

We loved this place. Ignore the bad reviews and appreciate that it’s an old school caravan park. Right up our alley and we are sure many other travellers who spent their childhood holidays in caravan parks just like this. The customer service upon check-in and throughout our stay was excellent. Such lovely people (and their dog Stevie too :)) and we really wish them all the best as they had only started a week before. The location in this wonderful old fishing village opposite the jetty and sand dunes was amazing. Yes, the amenities are a little dated but they were clean and tidy. You can’t ask for much more than that. Definitely stock up on supplies before heading out to Fowlers Bay as there are no shops however the kiosk does have a small range of essentials in case you forget. We can’t recommend this park enough and hope to get back there when we are travelling back through the region!

Heard lots of positive things about Fowlers Bay, however the caravan park🙄 so we were quite surprised to see it was pretty untidy...overgrown  weeds on our site, camp kitchen was filthy, fire pit area looked like it hadnt been used for a while broken chairs, weeds, looked like it had been let go...the half round toilet block hadn't been cleaned  during our stay. Yuk...
Unfortunately the beach was covered in really thick,  high seaweed which was a shame, no walking in the beach...Fowlers bay for me was a huge disappointment...
Тихий и отдаленный. Можно разжигать fire pit

We recently stayed at the caravan park in Fowlers Bay and unfortunately had a very disappointing experience.

We had booked and paid for two nights, but honestly couldn’t leave quickly enough. The facilities were in very poor condition — the bathrooms were unhygienic, with a strong unpleasant smell, and the mop provided for use after showering appeared to have dirty, stagnant water that hadn’t been changed in a long time.

The odour from both the bathrooms and the fish cleaning station was overwhelming and made the stay quite uncomfortable.

It’s a real shame, as the location itself has so much potential, but the condition of the facilities significantly impacted our ability to enjoy the area.

I honestly don’t know how this place is still operating. There’s absolutely nothing here, and the whole park feels like it hasn’t had a proper clean in ages—not just the bathrooms (which were full of massive spiders and thick cobwebs), but everywhere you look.

To make matters worse, the fish cleaning station is smack in the middle of the park, and the smell is constant—from early morning right through to the evening. It completely ruins any chance of relaxing.

We couldn’t get out of there fast enough. Definitely wouldn’t return.
Some might find it a bit rough. But for its remote location and some of the best fishing it one I will return to.

Disappointing place. Run down and old facilities. Basin tap In toilet doesn't turn on. Cold water only in showers - the ones that worked. Shop is not stocked with items as on website. Take all you need with you. Even though the location is beautiful, I wouldn't return or recommend.

Friendly welcome. Amenities were fine. Camp fire next to camp kitchen.
Good selection of food and drinks to purchase from caravan park office.
More than reasonable $25 per night for 2 adults unpowered site.
Lovely long jetty to walk along. Must be a good fishing spot as there were people fishing off jetty.
Sand dunes looked like fun, can hire boards at caravan park to ride down dunes.
Undiscovered hidden gem. Loved the tranquilty.

Got here late in the afternoon and the reception was already closed but right outside the reception’s door there’s a sign with info about late check-ins without a booking, couldn’t see check out times anyway.
The camp is clean in general even if we found some rubbish around it (bottle caps, plastic bags and tin cans/lids) but that probably is a lack of manners from other patrons.
The kitchen is small but with everything one could need, the barbies were a bit dirty but it was overall okay.
There’s no boiler for the showers as early in the morning they were ice cold, not a big deal but for some might be.
The only thing I can say is that the owner (?) the morning after didn’t even say “hi” and just told us that the check out was at 10 and if we wanted to stay more we had to pay for another night.
It was 10.20, but again, we didn’t even know the check out time as it’s not mentioned anywhere, I found that very rude as it wasn’t about money just about friendliness, a few words and a different way to say things always goes a long distance.
Anyway Fowlers bay is stunning.

📍 Fowlers Bay
Absolutely blown away by how stunning this place is 🤍
Crystal clear water, white sand, and that peaceful, untouched coastline feel.
The caravan park is a little hidden gem too — simple, quiet and the kind of spot you never want to leave.
South Australia, you’re showing off again 🙌✨
Very close to shore, well maintained

The disappointment of our trip from Melbourne to Perth. We were here 6 yrs ago and I was obsessed with coming back. In fact, it would’ve been the highlight of our trip.

I completely understand water can be scarce, but why can some vans hook up and others not?
The claim to be environmentally sustainable means to be that. Water is needed, RV toilets needs dumping. No getting away from it. I feel that asking people to fill up before coming and not providing a dump point simply means that another location needs to deal with important issues, which defeats the objective and it just unbalances the problem.
Unfortunately, our site also looked like dog poo central. I removed a poo bag full of poo from our site. I know dogs do what they like, dog owners can be terrible picking up poo, but maybe an inspection of the sites daily before new arrivals can help?

Anyway, nice location.
Thickshakes were the best we have had around Australia so far
Just stopped for food. Super friendly and GREAT food that hits the spot!!!!

Great little park, close to the jetty.
Great fun driving through the sand dunes down to the beach.

The staff were friendly and helpful
Quiet n dark at night which I love
The place was pretty isolated but interesting- worth having a wonder around.
Facility is ok nothing fancy
Nice, clean,  camp kitchen.  Friendly staff. Rainwater only.

Been going to fowlers bay for about 25 years: great caravan park and great fishing:
Will be there again in 3 weeks: can't wait
This park is ok amenities need a good clean.
Do bad big sites a little run down but right on the beach
A nice place to stay for a couple of days but the limestone sand gets into everything and unfortunately for us the wind and rain never stopped...

Generator is noisy - might be less so in summer when solar panels might kick in.
Facilities are all decent, location is amazing

Nice little spot enjoyed our stay but the amenities really do need a complete rebuild very tight for showers and basin area very tight they were clean though and the relievers were checking them all the time.
We really enjoyed our stay here, quiet, clean and great price.. perfect bit of paradise
Clean and helpful owner's
Great little caravan park. Staff where friendly and helpful. Could see whales in the cove.  Stayed for 4 nights but had no luck fishing. Would recommend
Friendly helpful owners. Great coffee at the canteen. Enjoyed my stay and EP whale watching cruise. You live in an amazing place.
Compact park in a beautiful part of the world. All the basic amenities you need for a break from being on the road.
Wow - just wow

Hot, high pressure showers, easy check in and clean and orderly campsite. The location is right in the middle of a tiny town that has been maintained well with interp signs in front of almost every establishment, so there’s plenty to learn. Heaps of squid to catch from the jetty too. Most enjoyable stop at the end of the nullabor.

The owner (Simon?) made our stay exceptional. We were four blokes in 3 vehicles who rocked up without booking late in the day.
I offered to buy wood for a campfire... "leave it to me" he said. When we got back for a hot shower he had a raging 'log chimney' fire going. Excellent location and camp kitchen!
Thanks

There’s no personality on arrival.
It’s suggested the water is tank water but I reckon it’s bore water. It’s SMELLS!!!
There’s a private generator right on the fence behind sites 8-11.
It’s loud and full of fumes.
I suggest asking for a site on the opposite side of the park
Plenty of spaces for caravans close proximity to the beach. Although toilets were clean,they need an upgrade before I would give them a higher star rating for the money you pay.
Rustic but everything you need.
Great park, very secluded and off track
Great location near the jetty/pier for fishing and squidding. Very basic re facilities but clean. Man at the desk quite grumpy while lady on the phone was chirpy🤷‍♀️

The gent at this park is very abrupt and quite rude!!!!
Also found the amenities not very clean with quite a bit of mould around the base of the shower.
If there was another park to stay at I think this park might go bust!!!
Only thing going for it was where it's situated across the road from the jetty with lots of fishing.

I feel the response to my review is typical of the owners attitude towards the us!!
If you can't accept constructive criticism,  why ask for reviews.
Looking at your answer to my review above, you obviously have me mixed up with someone else. I never once washed my dog in the showers. Also we never asked to be moved near the beach, just to a site more comfortable for a 5 night stay!!!

Absolutely loved it. Affordable, the owners were great, very clean facility, fishing was awesome, 4wd thru the dunes. Lots of history to be seen. Wanted to stay longer but unfortunately couldn't. Thank you

We stayed at Fowlers Bay Caravan Park, which is only accessible via dirt roads. The village itself is very quiet, with no gas stations or much else around. However, the park is just a short walk from the beautiful pier by the sea, which was a nice touch.

There isn’t much to do in the area, and it’s quite exposed to the wind. The facilities were relatively clean, and the camp kitchen was functional and well-equipped, making it a decent spot for a short stay.

Fowlers Bay Caravan Park is a peaceful and remote gem, perfect for those looking to escape the hustle and bustle. It's situated close to the jetty, with a short drive to Scott Beach and the unique Mexican Hat, offering some great exploration spots. The facilities are clean and well-maintained, though the washing machine was out of order during our stay, which was a bit of an inconvenience.

The reception kiosk is handy, with a supply of fishing gear available for guests. Since there are no shops nearby, it’s best to stock up on food essentials before arriving or plan to catch your own from the jetty. You’ll find all sorts of fish, including squid and tommies. One thing to note is that there’s no petrol station in Fowlers Bay, so make sure to fill up at Penong or Nundroo, depending on your route. It’s a great spot for a quiet, off-the-beaten-path stay, perfect for fishing and relaxing in a remote coastal setting.
La heladería no enfría
Just stopped for 30 minutes for coffee, a friendly people asked where I was from and a short walk along the jetty

Great location on the waterfront with jetty right in front of caravan park. The kids had a ball on the sand dunes right behind the park. Facilities were good. We were camping in a tent. Camp area was dusty, but has shady trees and shelter from wind. Be aware generator is right behind camping area. Doesn't run overnight, but came on about 7.30 am and off 9pm.
What a fantastic caravan park.excellent location. Large van sites. Staff were very welcoming and helpful ..will be back

This is a great caravan Park.  The caretakers are really friendly and helpful.  We love staying here.  Big sites so you can feel relaxed and not squashed in. Definitely will be back and recommend it to everyone.  Be respectful of the water. It is liquid gold there.
Great spot, was very windy but a great experience.

As reviewers have already said, the park is basic but spotlessly clean.  Caretakers were friendly and obliging when our weird set-up would not fit in the standard unpowered site.  We came for one night and stayed five.  If able, drive the sand dunes to the sea lion colony on Point Fowler and visit Scott Bay.  If you're still feeling adventurous, drive to Scott Point and Mexican Hat.  So much to see and do, and I haven't even mentioned the fishing!
Could have been a bit cleaner than what it was!
Lovely park managers.  A very nice park.  Located close to the jetty.  We had great luck with fishing. Laundry was locked.
Would go there again was a great spot
Loved this place ❤️  small and quite. Good fishing on the jetty.
Good facility and price is spot on for what you get 👍
Nice getaway place squid where biting

What a spot, like a hidden gem. So nice with a great wharf to fish off, 4 wheel driving everywhere and a lovely lady Gracie at the counter. So helpful. The park is quiet and amenities are clean. There is only one washing machine and it is a good one. It's only a stones throw off beach. Small store with a few necessitys
Nice managers, good amenities and location. Coffee and toasties available from the kiosk.
great little hidden treasure if u want a quiet retreat this is the place

What a lovely beachside town, the caravan park is only small..we had plenty of room for our 22ft van.
Gracie who is in the office at the park is an absolute joy to deal with.
Went out with EP tours to see some whales which we did mum's & calves just brilliant. Don't pass Fowler's Bay by, stop and chill.
Road in is terrible & needs grading if you want people to visit. Facilities old but clean. Caravan sandy & worse when wet. Great location on the beach. Minimal food at kiosk  - bring your own.

We stayed a few days, and the service was great .
Facilities are basic but kept clean. The disabled bathroom is great, the toilet is raised for easy use , the shower is very roomy, and a real winner. There is a camp kitchen with gas BBQ.  Also, there is an area for a fire near the kitchen, and the park staff provides firewood.
Basic caravan park which survives on rainwater.  Clean and tidy, nice owner/ managers
Great location,  staff were very accommodating and helpful. Good fishing and plenty to see. A recommended spot👍👍
It is a great spot for fisherman for fishing but not my cup of tea

Been coming here for nearly 40 years, we enjoy the place, remember this place was a ghost town 50 years ago, this was the last outpost for bagged grain, no c/v park back then. The owners have done a great job in getting the park slowly upgraded, remember this area is out of council and most work is done by locals, the park makes it's own power and here now nothing has tripped, the water is from a soak (soft), as the park is full most days it says it all ,come and enjoy. Ps. I'm 90 so have seen it all 🍷🍷😀.
Great place to stay. No mains power or water, park relys on solar and diesel for power, and bore for water. Decent amenities, great jetty nearby and sand dunes for 4wd.
Nice quiet location.
Great little park. Clean and love the kitchen and fire pit area
It’s a nice quiet place.They say Whales were seen there.They usually come during May.

Gorgeous little town,  great place to do some fishing, spend time in nature and just relax. Local beaches are amazing, jetty is straight across the road from the park, making this park the perfect fishing and camping spot
Caravan park was great, exactly what we expected for the location. Clean, tidy,  friendly staff and good size sites.
Thoroughly enjoyed our stay here and would visit again.
Fowlers Bay is great. Go to Scotts Beach and Mexican hat. Caravan park  is ok just a bit tired.
Worth coming to the kiosk just for the chips after the slew of roadhouses. The bay has some rich SA history to discover.
Lovely spot to stop when doing the Nullarbor, &/or if you're a keen fisherman
Always a hit with the kids. Squid, beach, sand dunes, and not many people!

A dirt road leading to a cosy caravan park. Right on the beach and very close to the jetty. Perfect beach for walking, skinny dipping, sunbathing. The best beach walks I have done in Australia was here. The water is shallow and you can walk in 100 meters and still not very deep. Soft and friendly waves.
The camping has water and power and also amenities. The office is located in a separate building, not straightforward to find it. They sell basic food supplies too.

Nice little town one block. Pretty picturesque for photos jetty old buildings and tractors and sand dunes.
I stayed in caravan park clean amenities friendly staff at kiosk check in. You can go in one way from highway then back out the other so great first stopover on Nullarbor.
Beautiful part of SA  to spend time in.

The town is harsh because of its remoteness and size but this caravan park provides a quiet refuge for travellers and anglers. They've got a good hot water system and reliable electricity. Great place for caravanners and dog-lovers.

Good amenities and close to the beach, but stink from the beach is a killer. Piles of rotting sea weed spread a terrible smell over the whole area. Also owners could improve their courtesy and attitude towards the visitors.

Restricted hours for laundry are a nuisance. The unisex amenities need some attention and I personally don't see the need for them. The gloss from the Ceduna Info Centre has not been realised. Obviously we were rather disappointed when it was built up so much.
very nice

Fantastic stay! A great place to stop in on your travels to check out the spectacular scenery and grab a coffee (highly highly recommend the coffee!) or spend a night. Staff are friendly and accommodating. Facilities are top notch, considering this place is completely off grid and in the elements. Shout out to Robyn and Simon for everything they do to make this place great
Great caravan park,
Great little detour of the Hwy. Awsome beach to walk miles along at low tide. Power and trickle fed water to your site. Massive sand dunes just behind the village threaten to engulf the lot.

What a great place, plenty of 4x4ing and places to see, and the fishing was great to, if you're looking for 5 star hotel type accommodation this is not the place for you but off grid comfortable caravan park nice and clean and a campfire put on for you every night to gather around and meet others was bloody fantastic, well done owners.
We stayed in a cabin very modern and clean staff friendly bit pricey but it's very remote so ok
Small park in a tiny village. Well worth the drive in.
Top spot, smaller park but good, clean and tidy
Great jetty to fish from  plenty of history here. Great sand dunes for 4wd.

Great place in a fantastic spot not for from the harbor and just over the sand dunes from the beach . An older park but has everything needed for a great stay . A short walk from a Cafe general store which is also the park office .

For an of the beaten track this place was pretty good , it was a tight squeeze for a bigger van like ours but we got in without any real problems, quiet, friendly , I would certainly go back there again

What a beautiful place, feels like the end of the earth. Fantastic little caravan park, amenities clean, owners friendly (you check in at the kiosk next door). Really appreciate that we can have power, toilets and hot water here in a remote location (off grid), near beach and sand dunes,, and from July whale watching.    Enjoyed the campfire,  lit near the camp kitchen each evening.   We’ll be back! 👍
